meaning: adj.缺乏营养的,贫瘠的,无兴趣的,不成熟的
examples: We knew we were in for a pretty long, jejune evening.
She seems a rather creepy, jejune and adolescent fantasy.
We were surprised by their jejune responses to our problems.
The tribalists badly underestimated Mr Cameron, blindly dismissing him as a jejune, upstart toff.
